LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CMEA DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED FOR REASONS STATED ON THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. IN ADDITON, THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO EXTEND TIME TO OBTAIN AN ORDER SEALING CERTAIN DOCUMENTS TO MAY 16, 2013. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE AN EX PARTE APPLICATION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

ORDER RE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DAWN-SHEMAIN WEEKS', MARGARET HINES', AND SHANNON SCHLAGENHAUF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY. THE COURT ADOPTS THE REASONING AND ANALYSIS IN PRO TEM JUDGE ROSENQUIST'S REPORT IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED, SUBJECT TO THE TERMS OF THE PARTIES' PREVIOUSLY STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER DESIGNATING THE HAAG SEPARATION AGREEMENT AND DOCUMENTS AND THE HANDELSMAN SEPARATION AGREEMENT AS "ATTORNEY EYES ONLY" - RESTRICTING DISCLOSURE OF THE DOCUMENTS INITIALLY TO COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES AND EXCLUDING THE PARTIES THEMSELVES UNLESS SUBSEQUENTLY ORDERED OR PERMITTED BY THE COURT. DEFENDANTS MAY REDACT PAYMENT INFORMATION PERTAINING SOLELY TO AMOUNTS PAID TO HAAG AND HANDELSMAN TO BUY THEIR EQUITY OR TO THE IDENTITY OF OTHER EQUITY OWNERS OF CMEA. DEFENDANTS MAY NOT REDACT PAYMENT INFORMATION MADE TO HAAG OR HANDELSMAN WITH RESPECT TO ANY PAYMENT MADE FOR DEFENSE COSTS OR INDEMNITY OF HAAG OR HANDELSMAN WITH RESPECT TO THE CASE AT BAR. IF DEFENDANTS MAKE OTHER REDACTIONS IN THE DOCUMENTS, EACH SUCH REDACTION MUST BE SUPPORTED BY A PRIVILEGE LOG SPECIFICALLY DESCRIBING THE REDACTION, THE GROUNDS FOR THE REDACTION, AND PROVIDING SUFFICIENT FACTUAL INFORMATION FOR THE COURT TO RULE UPON SUCH A REDACTION IF REQUESTED TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRISTOPHER LEBSOCK, ESQ., 415.633.1908, FOR PLAINTIFFS DAWN-SHERMAIN WEEKS et al; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: Plaintiffs DAWN-SHEMAIN WEEKS', MARGARET HINES', and SHANNON SCHLAGENHAUF'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery.
The Court adopts the reasoning and analysis in Pro Tem Judge Rosenquist's report in its entirety. The motion to compel is granted, subject to the terms of the parties' previously stipulated protective order designating the Haag separation agreement and documents and the Handelsman separation agreement as "Attorney Eyes Only" - restricting disclosure of the documents initially to counsel for the parties and excluding the parties themselves unless subsequently ordered or permitted by the court. Defendants may redact payment information pertaining solely to amounts paid to Haag and Handelsman to buy their equity or to the identity of other equity owners of CMEA. Defendants may not redact payment information made to Haag or Handelsman with respect to any payment made for defense costs or indemnity of Haag or Handelsman with respect to the case at bar. If defendants make other redactions in the documents, each such redaction must be supported by a privilege log specifically describing the redaction, the grounds for the redaction, and providing sufficient factual information for the court to rule upon such a redaction if requested to do so. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar May-09-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lara Villaruel Hutner, Esq. for Villaruel Hutner & Todd, Tel. 415.632.4101, for defendant and moving party CMEA;
Christopher L. Lebsock, Esq. of Hausfeld, Tel. 415.633.1908, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Marcie S. Isom, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendant John Haag.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT CMEA DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is denied for reasons stated on the record. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. In additon, the Court granted plaintiff's request to extend time to obtain an order sealing certain documents to May 16, 2013. Plaintiff may file an ex parte application. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
